itineraries.name: 3-day Northern Rhone: Cote-Rotie, Hermitage and Cornas
itineraries.theme: Northern Rhone trilogy: Syrah in three expressions on granite and schist

days.morning: Arrive in Ampuis (30 min south of Lyon) and visit E. Guigal for the La Doriane Condrieu and Cote-Rotie tasting. Book the private La La La tasting experience two months in advance for access to La Mouline, La Landonne, and La Turque barrel samples.
days.lunch: Lunch at the Guigal table at the Ampuis maison, or at a Vienne bouchon overlooking the Rhone.
days.afternoon: Drive to Condrieu village (10 min south of Ampuis) for a tasting at Georges Vernay. Focus on Coteau de Vernon single-parcel Viognier. Then continue south to Tain-l'Hermitage (40 min) to check into accommodation.
days.evening: Evening tasting at M. Chapoutier's cellar shop in Tain-l'Hermitage; the Rousanne Ermitage selection is poured by the glass.
days.accommodation: Hotel du Manoir at Tain-l'Hermitage or Le Bon Accueil on the Tournon bank.

days.morning: Morning climb of the Hermitage hill with M. Chapoutier's guided vineyard walk; see the terraced granitic parcels (Les Bessards, Le Meal, Les Greffieux) and the Saint-Christophe chapel at the summit.
days.lunch: Lunch at Maison Jaboulet in Tain-l'Hermitage, paired with a selection of Hermitage and Crozes-Hermitage.
days.afternoon: Afternoon tasting at Delas Freres (in Saint-Jean-de-Muzols, 5 min across the river) focused on the Hermitage Bessards single-parcel and Saint-Joseph range.
days.evening: Dinner in Tournon-sur-Rhone at a restaurant using Rhone Valley wines on the list.

days.morning: Drive 30 min south to Cornas village for a cellar visit with the Clape family at Domaine Auguste Clape. The estate requires an appointment; the Cornas old-vine cuvee is the focus. Also visit Domaine du Tunnel in Saint-Peray (10 min away) for sparkling Marsanne.
days.lunch: Lunch in Valence (20 min south) at Maison Pic, a three-Michelin-star restaurant with one of the Rhone's finest wine cellars. Book weeks in advance.
days.afternoon: Visit the Valence wine shop Cave de Pic, operated by the Pic family, for a comprehensive Rhone and Valence region selection. Then drive back to Lyon (1h) or continue south.

itineraries.description: Three-day drive through the core northern Rhone appellations: Cote-Rotie and Condrieu from Ampuis, Hermitage and Crozes-Hermitage from Tain, and Cornas from the southern granite zone. The purest Syrah expression in the world across three days.
itineraries.tip: All four cellar visits require advance booking. Book Guigal and Chapoutier two months ahead; Clape is by personal referral or appointment request only.

itineraries.name: 3-day Southern Rhone: Chateauneuf-du-Pape, Gigondas and Tavel
itineraries.theme: Southern Rhone Grenache: the galets, the garrigue, and the papal appellation

days.morning: Drive from Avignon to Chateauneuf-du-Pape (20 min). Morning visit to Chateau de Beaucastel by appointment, focusing on the Mourvèdre-dominant Hommage a Jacques Perrin and the Roussanne Vieilles Vignes blanc. Tour the biodynamic vineyards.
days.lunch: Lunch in the Chateauneuf-du-Pape village at Le Pistou or La Mere Germaine, with a carafe of the local commune's rouge.
days.afternoon: Afternoon tasting at Domaine du Vieux Telegraphe in Bedarrides (10 min). Focus on La Crau and the standard blend; the estate offers walk-in tastings at the cellar door.
days.evening: Return to Avignon for dinner. Wine bar on Place Crillon for a by-the-glass Cotes du Rhone survey.
days.accommodation: Avignon centre or La Mirande (historic palace hotel).

days.morning: Drive to Gigondas (45 min from Avignon). Morning hike in the Dentelles de Montmirail (1-2 hours), then tasting at Domaine Santa Duc (book in advance). The Hautes Garrigues cuvee is the focus.
days.lunch: Lunch at the Florets restaurant in Gigondas, which serves a serious list of Gigondas and Vacqueyras with local Provencal cuisine.
days.afternoon: Short drive to Vacqueyras (10 min) for a tasting at the Clos des Cazeaux; then to Beaumes-de-Venise (15 min) for a Muscat VDN tasting at the Cave de Beaumes-de-Venise.
days.evening: Return to Avignon. Evening walk through the Palais des Papes quarter to connect the papal wine history with the day's Grenache tasting.

days.morning: Drive to Tavel (15 min from Avignon). Morning tasting at Domaine de la Mordoree (book in advance); the Tavel, Lirac rouge, and Chateauneuf-du-Pape cuvees of the estate are the highlights.
days.lunch: Lunch at the Prieure de Montezargues restaurant near Tavel, or return to Avignon's covered market for regional produce and a glass.
days.afternoon: Drive to Lirac (5 min from Tavel) for Chateau d'Aqueria tasting; the blanc and rouge of Lirac AOC offer the right-bank Rhone character at very accessible prices. Then drive back to Avignon.
days.evening: Farewell dinner in Avignon at a restaurant with a comprehensive southern Rhone cellar.

itineraries.description: Three-day southern Rhone exploration from an Avignon base: Chateauneuf-du-Pape biodynamic estates on day one, the Dentelles and Gigondas-Vacqueyras cluster on day two, and Tavel-Lirac rose country on day three.
itineraries.tip: Beaucastel and Mordoree both require advance booking. The driving distances are short but the hills east of Avignon are best explored in the cooler morning hours in summer.

itineraries.name: 5-day Grand Rhone: Lyon to Avignon, north to south
itineraries.theme: The complete Rhone corridor: Syrah to Grenache, from Lyon to the Palais des Papes

days.morning: Arrive in Lyon and check in. Morning: visit Vieux-Lyon bouchons for a Cotes du Rhone and Beaujolais orientation. The bouchon tradition (informal Lyonnaise restaurants) pairs northern Rhone wine with charcuterie and quenelles.
days.lunch: Lunch at a Vieux-Lyon bouchon; order a pichet of Saint-Joseph or Cotes du Rhone rouge.
days.afternoon: Afternoon: drive south to Ampuis (30 min) for a Guigal Cote-Rotie tasting and return to Lyon for dinner.
days.evening: Dinner in Lyon's Presqu'ile. Wine bar L'Oenomane or Winebar Les Bacchantes for a northern Rhone by-the-glass selection.

days.morning: Full day in the northern Rhone. Morning: Georges Vernay in Condrieu for Viognier.
days.lunch: Lunch in Condrieu village at a riverside brasserie.
days.afternoon: Drive to Cornas and Saint-Peray for tastings at Domaine du Tunnel (book in advance).
days.evening: Drive to Tain-l'Hermitage and check in. Evening: M. Chapoutier cellar shop.

days.morning: Hermitage hill vineyard walk with Chapoutier guide. Then morning tasting at Maison Paul Jaboulet Aine for La Chapelle.
days.lunch: Lunch in Tain-l'Hermitage. Valrhona chocolate shop nearby for a post-lunch detour.
days.afternoon: Afternoon at Delas Freres in Tournon. Drive south toward Avignon (2h).
days.evening: Arrive Avignon. Check in and orientation walk through the Palais des Papes quarter.

days.morning: Chateauneuf-du-Pape: morning tasting at Beaucastel (appointment required).
days.afternoon: Vieux Telegraphe in Bedarrides. Then Gigondas (40 min) for Santa Duc.
days.evening: Return to Avignon. Dinner at a restaurant with a full Rhone cellar.

days.morning: Morning: Tavel and Domaine de la Mordoree. Then Roman theatre at Orange (30 min).
days.lunch: Lunch at Le Parvis in Orange near the Roman theatre.
days.afternoon: Drive to Avignon for departure, or extend to Rasteau for the VDN cellar (20 min north of Orange).

itineraries.description: Five-day north-to-south Rhone drive from Lyon to Avignon covering Cote-Rotie, Condrieu, Hermitage, Cornas, Chateauneuf-du-Pape, Gigondas, and Tavel. The definitive introduction to the full Rhone Valley wine corridor.
itineraries.tip: Book all cellar visits at least three weeks in advance. A rental car from Lyon is essential; the TGV connects Lyon and Avignon in 1h for those arriving separately to start and end the trip.

itineraries.name: Hermitage hill deep-dive: 2 days in Tain-l'Hermitage
itineraries.theme: Syrah on granite: the full Hermitage hill repertoire from cask to glass

days.morning: Early-morning vineyard walk on the Hermitage hill before the summer heat. The marked trail starts behind the Chapoutier cellar and reaches the Saint-Christophe chapel in 45 minutes. Views over the Rhone to Tournon and the Vercors are spectacular.
days.lunch: Lunch at the Chapoutier tasting salon (La Table de Chapoutier).
days.afternoon: Extended tasting session at M. Chapoutier: Crozes-Hermitage through to the single-parcel Hermitage cuvees (L'Ermite, Le Pavillon, Le Meal, La Sizeranne) and the white Ermitage de l'Oree. All biodynamic.
days.evening: Dinner at Maison Jaboulet's tasting table, paired with the La Chapelle vertical.
days.accommodation: Hotel du Manoir or Les Terrasses de l'Hermitage in Tain.

days.morning: Morning tasting at Delas Freres in Tournon for the Les Bessards single-parcel Hermitage and Hermitage blanc Maison Blanche.
days.lunch: Lunch at a riverside restaurant in Tournon with views of the Hermitage hill.
days.afternoon: Optional visit to independent growers: Marc Sorrel (small estate, granite parcels; appointment required) and Jean-Louis Chave (appointments extremely limited, mostly trade allocation).
days.evening: Final tasting at Chapoutier cellar shop before evening departure.

itineraries.description: Two-day immersion in the Hermitage hill: vineyard walks, cellar visits at the three major houses, and the exploration of Syrah on granite from entry-level Crozes to the grand single-parcel Hermitage cuvees.
itineraries.tip: The Hermitage hill is a UNESCO candidate site. The single-parcel tastings at Chapoutier and the La Chapelle vertical at Jaboulet are the most analytically rich tastings in the northern Rhone.

verified.address_quoted: 18 avenue du Docteur Paul Durand, 26600 Tain-l'Hermitage

itineraries.name: Dentelles de Montmirail weekend: Gigondas, Vacqueyras and Beaumes-de-Venise
itineraries.theme: Southern Rhone villages: Grenache at altitude under the Dentelles limestone

days.morning: Morning hike in the Dentelles de Montmirail from the Gigondas trailhead: the 3-hour circuit through lavender garrigue and limestone pinnacles. Views over the vineyards to Chateauneuf-du-Pape in the distance.
days.lunch: Post-hike lunch at Les Florets restaurant in Gigondas, which has a sommelier-curated Gigondas list and terrace overlooking the Dentelles.
days.afternoon: Afternoon tastings in Gigondas village: Domaine Santa Duc (appointment recommended), Domaine des Pallieroudas, and the local cave cooperative.
days.evening: Short drive to Vaison-la-Romaine for dinner and overnight. Vaison's Roman ruins and medieval upper city are worth an evening exploration.

days.morning: Morning visit to Vacqueyras producers: Clos des Cazeaux and Le Sang des Cailloux (terraced old-vine Grenache; appointment recommended). Both estates can be visited on a single morning.
days.afternoon: Afternoon visit to the Cave de Beaumes-de-Venise for a Muscat de Beaumes-de-Venise VDN tasting and a tour of the Beaumes-de-Venise rouge (the appellation's dry red, often overlooked). Then drive back to Avignon or Orange for departure.
days.evening: Return via Orange for the Roman theatre, or depart from Avignon TGV.

itineraries.description: Two-day weekend in the southern Rhone's most scenic wine-tourism cluster: hiking the Dentelles, Grenache tastings in Gigondas and Vacqueyras, and the Muscat de Beaumes-de-Venise fortified finale.
itineraries.tip: Les Florets is the best meal in Gigondas. Book in advance and ask for the terrace table. The cave cooperative in Gigondas sells all appellation styles at cellar-door prices.
